title: cxxrtl: eliding wires connected to a $memwr impacts model behaviour

features.name: memory write with process-driven enable wire
features.description: Code should include a memory write operation where the enable signal is driven by a process (initial block with sync init) rather than directly from a register output

features.name: memory write port with replicated enable signal
features.description: Code should include a memory write where the enable signal is replicated to match the memory width (8 bits in this case)
features.snippet:
wire [7:0] en_replicated = {8{en}};
memory mem (en_replicated, addr, data, clk);

features.name: multiple processes driving memory write control signals
features.description: Code should include multiple separate initial/always processes that drive different memory write control signals (address, enable, data) which then feed into a single memory write port

features.name: wire assignment chain from input to memory port
features.description: Code should include a chain of wire assignments where input ports are assigned to intermediate wires which are then connected to memory write ports
